notes: Reopening runway — how long each friction persists after de-escalation, anchored to the historical record in events.json and the insurance ledger. Ranges are estimates from public reporting; the point of the panel is that d→0 on the slider is not day zero in the market.

phases.summary: Threat premiums mean-revert within weeks once barrels flow — unless barrels stay missing.

anchors.text: Abqaiq 2019: +14.6% one-day spike fully round-tripped in under a month despite 5.7 M b/d offline
Soleimani 2020: +3.6% unwound in about two weeks
Twelve-day war 2025: −7% on the ceasefire headline alone

phases.summary: Underwriters normalize on renewal cycles and JWC reviews, not headlines — premiums stay multiples of the old baseline for months to years.

anchors.text: After the 2019 spiral, Gulf rates held ~10× their April-2019 level well into 2021
The 'pre-crisis' 0.25% baseline of Feb 2026 was itself 5–25× the 2018 norm — the 2019 shock never fully unwound
anchors.source: This site's insurance ledger (S&P Global; Strauss Center)

phases.summary: A swept convoy corridor takes months; certified full clearance takes years. Mines outlast every ceasefire that laid them.

anchors.text: 1991 Gulf War: 1,200+ mines; coalition clearance operations ran for over two years
1987–88: Earnest Will convoys ran under mine threat for months; Bridgeton struck a mine inside an escorted convoy
Mid-2026: ~80 mines reported still blocking the central corridor — clearance had not begun in earnest as of this build
